title: Can I Get an MOT Without a V5C? Yes, Here's How in the UK
metaDescription: Yes, you can get an MOT without your V5C logbook in the UK. Discover what you *do* need for the test, why the V5C is important, and what to do if you don't have it.
targetKeyword: can i get an mot without a v5c
content:
Yes, you **can get an MOT without a V5C** (Vehicle Registration Certificate) in the UK. While the V5C logbook is crucial for proving ownership and registering your car, MOT test centres only need your vehicle's registration number to carry out the inspection. This means your car can still pass its MOT, even if your V5C is missing or hasn't arrived yet.

## You Don't Need Your V5C for the MOT Test Itself

When your car goes for its MOT, the garage's main focus is its roadworthiness. They check safety, emissions, and structural integrity. You don't need to present your V5C document.

The test centre simply needs your vehicle's registration number (VRN). This allows them to log the results accurately with the DVSA. They won't ask to see your logbook.

### Why the Confusion? The V5C's True Importance

Many people ask "can I get an MOT without a V5C" because it's such a vital document. The V5C proves you're the registered keeper of your car. It holds key details like make, model, engine size, and chassis number.

You need it for tasks like taxing your car, selling it, or changing its details with the DVLA. Its absence can cause problems in other areas, but not for the physical MOT test.

## Common Reasons You Might Not Have a V5C

It's quite common for car owners to find themselves without a V5C at hand. Here are the main reasons:

### 1. You've Just Bought Your Car

Congratulations on your new wheels! If you've recently bought a car, especially from a private seller, you'll be waiting for the DVLA to send your new V5C. This can take a few weeks. Your MOT might be due during this waiting period.

### 2. Your V5C is Lost, Stolen, or Damaged

Life happens. Documents get misplaced, stolen, or simply wear out over time. If your V5C is missing, you'll need to apply for a replacement. This process can take time.

### 3. It's a Project or Unregistered Vehicle

For those working on a classic car restoration or a custom build, the V5C might not be in your name yet. Or, the vehicle may have been off the road for a long time. Its paperwork could be missing.

## What You *Absolutely* Need for the MOT Test

Forget the V5C for a moment. For the MOT inspection itself, you only need two things:

*   **Your vehicle:** Ready to be tested!
*   **The vehicle's registration number (VRN):** This is essential. The garage uses it to record the test with the DVSA. You can find your VRN on number plates, old MOT certificates, or insurance documents.

## Passed Your MOT Without a V5C? Here's What's Next

Great news! Your car passed its MOT, even without you needing your V5C. You'll receive a 'pass' certificate. This confirms your vehicle is roadworthy for another year.

However, remember that an MOT pass doesn't mean you can immediately drive the car legally. You still need valid car insurance and vehicle tax.

You can usually tax your car online using the 11-digit reference number from your new keeper slip (if you just bought it) or the 16-digit reference from a V11 reminder. If you have neither, you'll need your V5C.

## What to Do If You Don't Have Your V5C Logbook

If your V5C is lost, stolen, or damaged, don't panic. You can apply for a replacement from the DVLA.

**Applying for a new V5C:** You'll need to complete a V62 form. Send this to the DVLA with the required fee (currently £25). You can download the V62 form from GOV.UK.

**New Keeper Slip (V5C/2):** If you've just bought a car and have the green 'new keeper slip' (section 2 of the V5C), you can use this to tax the vehicle while you wait for your full V5C. This slip is also proof of your pending registration.

Waiting for a V5C can take several weeks, so apply as soon as you realise it's missing. For more detailed advice, check out our helpful [guides](/guides).

## Frequently Asked Questions: Can I Get an MOT Without a V5C?

**Q: Can I tax my car without a V5C after an MOT?**
A: You generally need your V5C or the new keeper slip (V5C/2) to tax your car. While your MOT can proceed without the V5C, taxing it legally will be difficult without the correct document reference. Apply for a replacement V5C using a V62 form if you don't have one.

**Q: Will an MOT garage refuse to test my car without a V5C?**
A: No, an MOT test centre should not refuse to test your car simply because you don't have the V5C. They only need the vehicle's registration number. If a garage refuses, consider finding another one. You can easily find an MOT centre and check your car's MOT history with our [Free MOT Checker](/mot-checker).

**Q: What if I lose my V5C immediately after buying a car?**
A: If you have the green 'new keeper slip' (V5C/2) from the seller, you can use this to tax the car and prove you're the new keeper. Then, apply for a full replacement V5C using a V62 form.
